sample_note: Representative sample covering diverse patterns — GitHub uses all HTTP methods correctly

summaries.DELETE /app/installations/{installation_: Delete an installation for the authenticated app
summaries.GET /app/hook/config: Get a webhook configuration for an app
summaries.GET /app/hook/deliveries: List deliveries for an app webhook
summaries.GET /app/hook/deliveries/{delivery_id}: Get a delivery for an app webhook
summaries.GET /app/installations: List installations for the authenticated app
summaries.GET /app/installations/{installation_id}: Get an installation for the authenticated app
summaries.PATCH /app/hook/config: Update a webhook configuration for an app
summaries.POST /app-manifests/{code}/conversions: Create a GitHub App from a manifest
summaries.POST /app/hook/deliveries/{delivery_id}/: Redeliver a delivery for an app webhook
summaries.POST /app/installations/{installation_id: Create an installation access token for an app
summaries.POST /user/repos: Create a repository for the authenticated user

descriptions.DELETE /app/installations/{installation_: Uninstalls a GitHub App on a user, organization, or enterprise account.
Removes a GitHub App installation suspension.
descriptions.DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}: Deleting a repository requires admin access.
descriptions.DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborato: Removes a collaborator from a repository.
descriptions.GET /: Get Hypermedia links to resources accessible in GitHub's REST API
descriptions.GET /advisories: Lists all global security advisories that match the specified parameters.
descriptions.GET /advisories/{ghsa_id}: Gets a global security advisory using its GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) identifier.
descriptions.GET /app: Returns the GitHub App associated with the authentication credentials used.
descriptions.GET /app/hook/config: Returns the webhook configuration for a GitHub App.
descriptions.GET /app/hook/deliveries: Returns a list of webhook deliveries for the webhook configured for a GitHub App.
descriptions.GET /app/hook/deliveries/{delivery_id}: Returns a delivery for the webhook configured for a GitHub App.
descriptions.GET /app/installations: The permissions the installation has are included under the `permissions` key.
descriptions.GET /app/installations/{installation_id}: Enables an authenticated GitHub App to find an installation's information using the installation id.
descriptions.GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}: The `parent` and `source` objects are present when the repository is a fork.
descriptions.GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators: For organization-owned repositories, the list of collaborators includes outside collaborators, organization members that are direct collaborators, organization members with access through team memberships, organization members with access through default organization permissions, and organization owners.
descriptions.GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_:
The API returns a [`301 Moved Permanently` status](https://docs.github.com/rest/guides/best-practices-for-using-the-rest-api#follow-redirects) if the issue was
[transferred](https://docs.github.com/articles/transferring-an-issue-to-another-repository/) to another repository.
You can use the REST API to list comments on issues and pull requests.
descriptions.GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls: Lists pull requests in a specified repository.
descriptions.GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_nu: Draft pull requests are available in public repositories with GitHub Free and GitHub Free for organizations, GitHub Pro, and legacy per-repository billing plans, and in public and private repositories with GitHub Team and GitHub Enterprise Cloud.
descriptions.GET /user: OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the `user` scope in order for the response to include private profile information.
descriptions.GET /users: Lists all users, in the order that they signed up on GitHub.
descriptions.GET /users/{username}: Provides publicly available information about someone with a GitHub account.
descriptions.GET /users/{username}/repos: Lists public repositories for the specified user.
descriptions.PATCH /app/hook/config: Updates the webhook configuration for a GitHub App.
descriptions.PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}: **Note**: To edit a repository's topics, use the [Replace all repository topics](https://docs.github.com/rest/repos/repos#replace-all-repository-topics) endpoint.
descriptions.PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issu: Issue owners and users with push access or Triage role can edit an issue.
descriptions.PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_: Draft pull requests are available in public repositories with GitHub Free and GitHub Free for organizations, GitHub Pro, and legacy per-repository billing plans, and in public and private repositories with GitHub Team and GitHub Enterprise Cloud.
descriptions.POST /app-manifests/{code}/conversions: Use this endpoint to complete the handshake necessary when implementing the [GitHub App Manifest flow](https://docs.github.com/apps/building-github-apps/creating-github-apps-from-a-manifest/).
descriptions.POST /app/hook/deliveries/{delivery_id}/: Redeliver a delivery for the webhook configured for a GitHub App.
descriptions.POST /app/installations/{installation_id: Creates an installation access token that enables a GitHub App to make authenticated API requests for the app's installation on an organization or individual account.
descriptions.POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues: Any user with pull access to a repository can create an issue.
descriptions.POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue: You can use the REST API to create comments on issues and pull requests.
descriptions.POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls: Draft pull requests are available in public repositories with GitHub Free and GitHub Free for organizations, GitHub Pro, and legacy per-repository billing plans, and in public and private repositories with GitHub Team and GitHub Enterprise Cloud.
descriptions.POST /user/repos: Creates a new repository for the authenticated user.
descriptions.PUT /app/installations/{installation_id}: Suspends a GitHub App on a user, organization, or enterprise account, which blocks the app from accessing the account's resources.
descriptions.PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/: Add a user to a repository with a specified level of access.
descriptions.PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_nu: Merges a pull request into the base branch.

notes.DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborato: Revised: generic→delete. DELETE on item-level resource = delete
notes.GET /: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. API root returns a single discovery object with hypermedia links, not a collection.
notes.GET /app: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton — returns the single authenticated app, summary says 'Get the authenticated app'.
notes.GET /app/hook/config: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton config resource — summary says 'Get a webhook configuration for an app'. Heuristic misclassifies as list because path has no ID parameter.
notes.GET /user: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton — returns the single authenticated user, operationId: users/get-authenticated.
notes.PATCH /app/hook/config: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update a webhook configuration for an app', operationId contains 'update'. Heuristic misclassifies as generic because PATCH on a path without ID parameter.
notes.POST /app-manifests/{code}/conversions: Audit: revised generic→create. operationId: apps/create-from-manifest, summary: 'Create a GitHub App from a manifest'. Both explicitly say create.
notes.PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/: Revised: generic→update. PUT on item-level resource = update
notes.PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_nu: Merge a PR (action, not standard CRUD)
